assertions.rationale: This specific property name is Duende-specific and not general OAuth/OIDC knowledge.

assertions.assertion: Client uses standard shared secret authentication (not certificate-based authentication)
assertions.rationale: The concept of separating token binding from client auth is in mTLS specs (RFC 8705).

assertions.assertion: Explains that the cnf claim with x5t#S256 will be embedded in the access token
assertions.rationale: While RFC 8705 defines x5t#S256, connecting it to Duende's specific configuration for non-auth binding is unlikely without the skill.

assertions.assertion: Mentions that the client must use a TLS client certificate when calling the token endpoint (even though the certificate is not used for authentication)
assertions.rationale: This nuance of Duende's implementation is not reliably known without specific documentation.
